---
title: "OSMnx · Measure accessibility"
---

::: {.lead}
Generate walking isochrones around a point in Burjassot, extract a single-radius
neighborhood, and cluster nodes by network distance.
:::

This walkthrough mirrors the
[Accessibility panel](../../panels/osmnx/accessibility.qmd).

## Before you begin

Download a `walk` network and project it (see
[Clean up the graph](graph-operations.qmd)).

## 1. Set the center

In **Accessibility**, choose the **Center Node** (use the eyedropper to click a
node in the viewport, e.g. the town center).

![Selecting the isochrone center node.](../../../assets/guides/osmnx/05-center.png)

## 2. Isochrones

Set:

- **Trip Times**: `5, 10, 15, 20` (minutes).
- **Travel Speed**: `4.5` km/h (walking).
- **Mode**: **Buffer Union** (smooth blobs) or **Convex Hull** (simpler shapes).

Press **Isochrones**. Nested polygons show how far you can walk in each time
budget.

![Nested walking isochrones around the center.](../../../assets/guides/osmnx/05-isochrones.png)

## 3. Ego subgraph

Press **Ego Subgraph** to extract the sub-network within a network-distance
radius of the center node — handy for isolating a neighborhood.

![The ego subgraph around the center node.](../../../assets/guides/osmnx/05-ego.png)

## 4. Network DBSCAN

Press **Network DBSCAN** to cluster nodes by network distance using `eps_meters`
and `min_samples`. Clusters reveal pockets of dense connectivity.

![Network DBSCAN clusters coloured by label.](../../../assets/guides/osmnx/05-dbscan.png)

## Next steps

Continue with [Compute statistics](statistics.qmd) for morphometrics and street
orientation.
